name: Tech Tree
description: A structured research or unlock graph where players spend resources, meet prerequisites, and unlock units, buildings, upgrades, policies, or strategic options.
design_notes: Tech trees turn long-term planning into visible structure. The system should define prerequisite nodes, research transactions, unlock effects, branch exclusivity, migration behavior, and UI explanations for why a technology is available, blocked, queued, or completed.
design_purpose: Give players strategic direction and long-term planning choices.
Gate advanced units, economies, territory tools, or victory paths behind readable prerequisites.
Let designers pace complexity and specialization across a match or campaign.
player_fantasy: The player feels their faction is advancing from basic capabilities into a specialized strategic identity.

parameters.name: faction_id
parameters.description: Faction or player ruleset that owns the research graph.

parameters.name: tech_unlock_id
parameters.description: Technology node being researched or completed.

parameters.name: prerequisite_node_ids
parameters.description: Required completed nodes before this unlock can begin.

parameters.name: research_progress_points
parameters.description: Accumulated research progress toward the current node.

parameters.name: resource_spend_transaction_id
parameters.description: Idempotent transaction for resource payment or research allocation.

parameters.name: unlock_effect_id
parameters.description: Effect applied when the technology completes.

parameters.name: research_queue_id
parameters.description: Ordered queue that owns active and pending research.

edge_cases: A technology is removed or renamed in an update while a save has it queued.
Two prerequisite branches complete on the same tick and unlock a dependent node.
A player loses the building or territory required to continue active research.
A research refund or respec interacts with already-created units or buildings.
Networked players issue queue changes simultaneously for shared faction research.
common_bugs: Unlock effects apply when research starts instead of when it completes.
Research queue UI accepts a node but server validation rejects missing prerequisites.
Progress is stored by display name and breaks after localization or balance updates.
Refunds return resources but leave unlock effects active.
Replay desyncs because research completion and resource income ticks resolve in different order.
balancing_notes: Early tech choices should open options quickly; late tech choices can carry heavier commitment.
Mutually exclusive branches need enough information for players to understand what they are giving up.
Research pacing should account for income spikes, territory loss, and comeback paths.
accessibility_notes: Provide searchable tree views, prerequisite explanations, and clear blocked-state reasons.
Avoid communicating branch types only through color or tiny icons.
Let players inspect future unlocks without requiring memorization of tooltips.

implementation_notes.unity: Represent tech nodes as data assets and active research as player or faction state. Validate prerequisite_node_ids and resource_spend_transaction_id before queue changes, then apply unlock_effect_id exactly once at completion.
implementation_notes.godot: Use Resources for tech definitions and a graph model for prerequisites. Store research queue, progress, and completed ids in save data, and have UI query validation reasons rather than duplicating prerequisite logic.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use Data Assets for tech nodes and a replicated research component for multiplayer factions. Server authority should order queue edits, resource spending, and completion effects by simulation tick for replay safety.
implementation_notes.web: Keep the tech graph in versioned JSON and process research through a deterministic reducer. Persist completed ids, queue ids, and migration version so browser saves survive balance and content updates.

example_games.title: Civilization VI
example_games.note: Uses technology and civics trees for long-term strategic planning.

example_games.title: Age of Empires II
example_games.note: Uses research unlocks to advance units, buildings, and strategies.
